data.title: PAX Global Technology Announces Leadership Change
data.description:

The latest announcement is out from PAX Global Technology ( (HK:0327) ).

PAX Global Technology Limited has announced a leadership change with the resignation of Mr. Lu Jie as Executive Director and CEO, effective July 3, 2025, due to other work engagements. Mr. Nie Guoming, the current Chairman of the Board and Executive Director, will assume the role of CEO, bringing over 25 years of experience in the card payment industry to the position. This transition is expected to maintain the company’s strategic direction and operational stability.

More about PAX Global Technology

PAX Global Technology Limited operates in the card payment industry, providing technology solutions through its subsidiaries. The company focuses on strategic development and management of its operations, with a significant presence in the market since its founding in 2000.

The latest update is out from SigmaRoc ( (GB:SRC) ).

SigmaRoc PLC, a company involved in the construction materials sector, has announced a change in its major holdings. Conversant Capital LLC, based in Wilmington, Delaware, USA, has reduced its voting rights in SigmaRoc from 5.96% to 4.84%. This adjustment in holdings may impact the company’s shareholder dynamics and influence its strategic decisions moving forward.

Turkcell Iletisim ( (TKC) ) has shared an update.

On July 2, 2025, Turkcell announced the redemption of its financing bond with ISIN code TRFTCEL72513, which had a nominal amount of TRY 230,000,000 and a maturity of 97 days. The bond, which carried an annual simple interest rate of 46.00%, was issued through a private placement to qualified investors. This redemption is part of Turkcell’s strategic financial management and reflects its commitment to fulfilling its financial obligations, potentially strengthening its position in the domestic market.

The company primarily focuses on the Turkish market, offering a wide range of communication and technology solutions.

On July 3, 2025, Borr Drilling Limited announced the pricing of its public offering of 50 million common shares at $2.05 per share, aiming to raise $102.5 million. The proceeds are intended for general corporate purposes, including debt service and capital expenditures. The offering involves two settlements, with the first on July 7, 2025, and the second contingent on a special general meeting on August 6, 2025. This move is expected to bolster Borr Drilling’s financial position and support its operational strategies.

Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited ( (HK:1406) ) has shared an update.

Clarity Medical Group Holding Limited has established a Separate Special Committee in response to certain allegations against the company. The committee, chaired by Mr. Lee Kam Hung Lawrence, aims to address these issues, with Mr. Lee bringing extensive legal expertise from his tenure at Baker McKenzie. Trading of the company’s shares remains suspended, and stakeholders are advised to exercise caution.

An update from Kun Peng International ( (KPEA) ) is now available.

On June 10, 2025, Kun Peng International Ltd.’s subsidiary, King Eagle China, and King Eagle Tianjin, along with its shareholders, terminated their original variable interest entity (VIE) agreements due to changes in equity ownership. New VIE agreements were established to maintain King Eagle China’s control and economic benefits from King Eagle Tianjin’s operations, ensuring continuity in their business relationship.

An update from Synopsys ( (SNPS) ) is now available.

On July 2, 2025, Synopsys announced that the U.S. Department of Commerce’s Bureau of Industry and Security has rescinded export restrictions related to China, which were initially communicated on May 29, 2025. The company is now working to restore access to its products in China and is assessing the impact of these export restrictions on its business operations and financial performance.
